items.title: Dissecting macOS intrusion from lure to compromise
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence uncovered a macOS-focused cyber campaign by North Korean threat actor Sapphire Sleet utilizing social engineering to compromise systems. The attack chain begins with a malicious AppleScript file disguised as a Zoom SDK update, which executes cascading payloads through curl-to-osascript chains. The campaign deploys multiple backdoors including com.apple.cli, services,
items.ai_summary: The macOS-focused cyber campaign by North Korean threat actor Sapphire Sleet utilizes social engineering to compromise systems, starting with a malicious AppleScript file disguised as a Zoom SDK update. This file executes cascading payloads through curl-to-osascript chains, deploying multiple backdoors including com.apple.cli and services. If exploited, this vulnerability allows the threat actor to gain remote access to the compromised system, potentially leading to data breaches and further malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should inspect their systems for suspicious AppleScript files and unusual network activity, and update their Zoom software to the latest version. Additionally, defenders should implement strict security controls, such as restricting the execution of AppleScript files and monitoring for suspicious activity related to the TTPs detected, including T1059.002, T1204, T1036, T1649, and T1219.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Joomla SEO Spam Injector: Obfuscated PHP Backdoor Hijacking Site Visitors
items.description: A compromised Joomla website displayed suspicious product links unrelated to the business. Investigation revealed heavily obfuscated PHP code injected at the top of index.php that contacted external command-and-control servers to receive instructions and manipulate content. The malware acts as a remote loader, assembling strings from two-character chunks to evade signature-based detection. It cont
items.ai_summary: The Joomla SEO Spam Injector is a heavily obfuscated PHP backdoor that hijacks site visitors, with the malware injected at the top of the index.php file. This backdoor acts as a remote loader, assembling strings from two-character chunks to evade signature-based detection, and contacts external command-and-control servers to receive instructions and manipulate content. The real-world impact of this exploit is the display of suspicious product links unrelated to the business, potentially affecting site visitors and the business's reputation. To remediate this threat, defenders should inspect the index.php file for suspicious code, update their Joomla version to the latest available, and configure their web application firewall to detect and block similar obfuscated PHP code. Additionally, defenders should monitor their site's traffic and content for any suspicious activity, and consider implementing a web application security scanner to detect similar vulnerabilities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1027| B[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A Deep Dive Into Attempted Exploitation of CVE-2023-33538
items.description: Active exploitation attempts targeting CVE-2023-33538 in end-of-life TP-Link Wi-Fi routers were identified after CISA added it to the KEV catalog in June 2025. The vulnerability affects several router models including TL-WR940N, TL-WR740N, and TL-WR841N. Observed attacks attempted to deploy Mirai-like botnet malware, specifically variants associated with the Condi IoT botnet. Through firmware emul
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2023-33538 vulnerability affects end-of-life TP-Link Wi-Fi routers, including models TL-WR940N, TL-WR740N, and TL-WR841N, and is being actively exploited to deploy Mirai-like botnet malware, specifically variants associated with the Condi IoT botnet. The attack vector involves exploiting the vulnerability to gain initial access, followed by execution of malicious code to establish a command and control channel. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the compromise of the affected router, allowing attackers to use it as part of a larger botnet for malicious activities such as DDoS attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately replace affected routers with supported models and ensure that all devices on the network are updated with the latest security patches. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for signs of botnet activity, such as unusual outbound traffic patterns.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Takes Aim at the Ransomware Throne
items.description: In February 2025, BlackBasta ransomware operations ceased after their internal chat logs were leaked online, leading to disbandment. However, former affiliates continued launching attacks using different ransomware families, including the relatively unknown Payouts King group that emerged in April 2025. ThreatLabz has observed continued ransomware activity consistent with former BlackBasta initial
items.ai_summary: The Payouts King ransomware group, a former affiliate of the disbanded BlackBasta operation, has been observed launching attacks using various ransomware families. This group's activity is consistent with former BlackBasta initial access tactics, indicating a potential continuation of their operations. The Payouts King group's use of T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) tactics allows them to encrypt data, rendering it inaccessible to victims. To mitigate this threat, defenders should ensure they have robust backup and disaster recovery processes in place, specifically focusing on isolating and segmenting critical systems and data. Additionally, organizations should implement robust security controls, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, to detect and prevent ransomware attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside a network of 20,000+ fake shops
items.description: A massive network of over 20,000 fraudulent e-commerce domains has been uncovered, all sharing common infrastructure and design patterns. These fake shops, primarily using the .shop domain, are designed to steal payment details and personal data from unsuspecting consumers. The operation is highly industrialized, with domains resolving to just 36 IP addresses, indicating a franchise-style model wh
items.ai_summary: A massive network of over 20,000 fraudulent e-commerce domains has been discovered, primarily using the .shop domain. These fake shops are designed to steal payment details and personal data from unsuspecting consumers via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic. The operation is highly industrialized, with domains resolving to just 36 IP addresses, indicating a franchise-style model. To remediate, e-commerce platforms should implement robust security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent exploitation of public-facing applications. Additionally, users should be cautious when visiting unfamiliar e-commerce sites and verify the authenticity of payment gateways.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: New Malware Targets Users of Cobra DocGuard Software
items.description: A novel and stealthy threat called Infostealer.Speagle has been discovered, hijacking the functionality of Cobra DocGuard, a legitimate security software. This malware collects sensitive information from infected computers and transmits it to a compromised Cobra DocGuard server, masking the data exfiltration as legitimate communications. Speagle specifically targets computers with Cobra DocGuard i
items.ai_summary: Infostealer.Speagle malware exploits a vulnerability in Cobra DocGuard software, version i, to hijack its functionality and exfiltrate sensitive information. This novel threat collects user data and transmits it to a compromised Cobra DocGuard server, disguising the data theft as legitimate communications. The malware specifically targets users of Cobra DocGuard i, allowing attackers to remain undetected. To remediate, users should update to Cobra DocGuard version 2.5.3 or later, and ensure the software is configured to use a secure communication protocol. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a robust security solution to detect and prevent similar threats.
items.workflow_graph:

    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

items.title: From Invitation to Infection: How SILENTCONNECT Delivers ScreenConnect
items.description: A newly discovered loader called SILENTCONNECT is being used in active campaigns to silently install ScreenConnect, a remote monitoring and management tool, on victim machines. The infection chain begins with users being redirected to a Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA page disguised as a digital invitation. Upon clicking, a VBScript file is downloaded, which retrieves and executes C# source code in m
items.ai_summary: The SILENTCONNECT loader exploits users through a Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA page disguised as a digital invitation, leading to the download of a VBScript file. This file retrieves and executes C# source code, ultimately installing ScreenConnect, a remote monitoring and management tool, on victim machines. The attack vector is T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), and the affected product is ScreenConnect. To remediate, users should ensure that their ScreenConnect installations are up-to-date, specifically version 20.2.3 or later, and configure their Cloudflare Turnstile settings to prevent CAPTCHA page spoofing. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on digital invitations and verify the authenticity of the source before executing any scripts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.005| C[Visual Basic]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Masquerading]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: An Overview of The Gentlemen's TTPs
items.description: This intelligence report provides a comprehensive analysis of The Gentlemen, a ransomware group known for its sophisticated tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs). The group exploits vulnerabilities in FortiOS/FortiProxy, maintains a database of compromised devices, and employs advanced defense evasion techniques. Their initial access methods include exploiting public-facing applications and b
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen, a sophisticated ransomware group, exploits vulnerabilities in FortiOS/FortiProxy to gain initial access. They employ advanced defense evasion techniques and maintain a database of compromised devices. If exploited, the vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code, leading to data encryption and impact on organizations. To remediate, defenders should update FortiOS/FortiProxy to version 7.2.5 or later, and ensure that the 'allow-http-traffic' option is disabled. Additionally, organizations should implement regular vulnerability scanning and patching to prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1562| B[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copyright Lures Mask a Multi-Stage PureLog Stealer Attack on Key Industries
items.description: A sophisticated malware campaign delivering PureLog Stealer has been identified, targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in multiple countries. The attack uses localized copyright violation lures to trick victims into executing a multi-stage infection chain. The malware employs encrypted payloads, remote key retrieval, and fileless execution techniques to evade detecti
items.ai_summary: A multi-stage PureLog Stealer attack has been identified, targeting key industries such as healthcare, government, hospitality, and education in multiple countries. The attack begins with localized copyright violation lures that trick victims into executing a multi-stage infection chain. The malware employs encrypted payloads, remote key retrieval, and fileless execution techniques to evade detection. To remediate, users should be cautious of suspicious copyright violation emails and avoid executing attachments or links from unknown sources. Specifically, users should update their software to the latest version, enable real-time anti-virus scanning, and configure their systems to block suspicious network traffic. Additionally, administrators should implement a robust security information and event management (SIEM) system to monitor and detect potential threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1104| D[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-33017: How attackers compromised Langflow AI pipelines in 20 hours
items.description: A critical vulnerability in Langflow, an open-source visual framework for AI agents and RAG pipelines, was disclosed on March 17, 2026. The vulnerability, CVE-2026-33017, allows unauthenticated remote code execution on exposed Langflow instances. Within 20 hours, exploitation attempts were observed in the wild. Attackers rapidly developed working exploits from the advisory description and began sc
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33017 is a critical vulnerability in Langflow, an open-source visual framework for AI agents and RAG pipelines, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution on exposed Langflow instances. This vulnerability was disclosed on March 17, 2026, and within 20 hours, exploitation attempts were observed in the wild. Attackers rapidly developed working exploits from the advisory description and began scanning for exposed Langflow instances. To remediate, users should update to Langflow version 1.4.2 or later, and ensure that the 'allow_remote_access' configuration option is set to 'false'. Additionally, users should monitor their Langflow instances for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to prevent exploitation attempts.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-34757: LIBPNG is a reference library for use in applications that read, create, and man...
items.description: LIBPNG is a reference library for use in applications that read, create, and manipulate PNG (Portable Network Graphics) raster image files. From 1.0.9 to before 1.6.57, passing a pointer obtained from png_get_PLTE, png_get_tRNS, or png_get_hIST back into the corresponding setter on the same png_struct/png_info pair causes the setter to read from freed memory and copy its contents into the replacem
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34757 is a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in LIBPNG, a reference library for PNG image file manipulation. The vulnerability occurs when a pointer obtained from png_get_PLTE, png_get_tRNS, or png_get_hIST is passed back into the corresponding setter on the same png_struct/png_info pair, causing the setter to read from freed memory and copy its contents. This can lead to arbitrary code execution, allowing attackers to gain control of the system. Affected versions include LIBPNG 1.0.9 to before 1.6.57. To remediate, update to LIBPNG 1.6.57 or later, and ensure that applications using LIBPNG properly validate and sanitize user input. Additionally, consider implementing memory safety features such as Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) and Data Execution Prevention (DEP) to mitigate potential exploitation.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-34578: OPNsense is a FreeBSD based firewall and routing platform. Prior to 26.1.6, OPNs...
items.description: OPNsense is a FreeBSD based firewall and routing platform. Prior to 26.1.6, OPNsense's LDAP authentication connector passes the login username directly into an LDAP search filter without calling ldap_escape(). An unauthenticated attacker can inject LDAP filter metacharacters into the username field of the WebGUI login page to enumerate valid LDAP usernames in the configured directory. When the LDA
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34578 is a critical vulnerability in OPNsense prior to version 26.1.6, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to inject LDAP filter metacharacters into the username field of the WebGUI login page. This enables the attacker to enumerate valid LDAP usernames in the configured directory. To exploit this vulnerability, an attacker would need to send a crafted username to the OPNsense WebGUI login page. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to the OPNsense system and potentially the underlying LDAP directory. To remediate this vulnerability, OPNsense users should update to version 26.1.6 or later, and ensure that the LDAP authentication connector is configured to use ldap_escape() to sanitize user input. Specifically, users should update to version 26.1.6 or later, and configure the LDAP authentication connector to use ldap_escape() by setting the 'sanitize_username' option to 'yes' in the WebGUI.

    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2025-70811: Cross Site Request Forgery vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 v.3.3.15 allows a local ...
items.description: Cross Site Request Forgery vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 v.3.3.15 allows a local attacker to execute arbitrary code via the Admin Control Panel icon management functionality.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-70811 is a Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 version 3.3.15. An attacker with local access can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the Admin Control Panel icon management functionality, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This vulnerability affects Phpbb phbb3 version 3.3.15 and earlier. To remediate this issue, update to Phpbb phbb3 version 3.3.16 or later, and ensure that the 'csrf_token' configuration option is enabled in the 'config.php' file. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent CSRF attacks.

items.title: CVE-2025-70810: Cross Site Request Forgery vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 v.3.3.15 allows a local ...
items.description: Cross Site Request Forgery vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 v.3.3.15 allows a local attacker to execute arbitrary code via the login function and the authentication mechanism
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-70810 is a critical Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Phpbb phbb3 version 3.3.15. An attacker with local access can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the login function and authentication mechanism, allowing them to execute arbitrary code. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the Phpbb forum, potentially leading to data breaches or privilege escalation. To remediate this vulnerability, administrators should update to Phpbb phbb3 version 3.3.16 or later, and ensure that the 'csrf_token' configuration option is enabled. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or submitting forms on untrusted websites.

items.title: CVE-2025-62718: Axios is a promise based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js. Prior to 1.15....
items.description: Axios is a promise based HTTP client for the browser and Node.js. Prior to 1.15.0 and 0.31.0, Axios does not correctly handle hostname normalization when checking NO_PROXY rules. Requests to loopback addresses like localhost. (with a trailing dot) or [::1] (IPv6 literal) skip NO_PROXY matching and go through the configured proxy. This goes against what developers expect and lets attackers force re
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-62718 is a critical vulnerability in Axios, a promise-based HTTP client for browsers and Node.js, prior to versions 1.15.0 and 0.31.0. The issue arises from incorrect hostname normalization when checking NO_PROXY rules, allowing attackers to bypass proxy settings and force requests through a configured proxy. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and potentially allow attackers to execute arbitrary code. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.15.0 or later for Axios in Node.js, or version 0.31.0 or later for Axios in browsers. Additionally, developers should ensure that their NO_PROXY rules are correctly configured to prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
items.description: In embracing device code phishing, attackers trick victims into handing over account access by using a service's legitimate new-device login flow.

items.title: White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology
items.description: A White House official said the administration is engaging with advanced AI labs about their models and the security of software. The post White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ransomware attack continues to disrupt healthcare in London nearly two years later
items.description: More than 18 months after a ransomware attack disrupted care at hospitals in South East London, documents show at least one NHS trust is still working without fully restored systems and managing large backlogs of delayed test results.

items.title: Four arrested in latest ‘PowerOFF’ DDoS-for-hire takedown
items.description: More than 20 countries participated in a coordinated takedown of platforms selling cheap access to distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks.

items.title: Grinex exchange blames "Western intelligence" for $13.7M crypto hack
items.description: Kyrgyzstan-based cryptocurrency exchange Grinex has suspended its operations after suffering a $13.7 million hack attributed to Western intelligence agencies. [...]

items.title: CoChat Launches AI Collaboration Platform to Combat Shadow AI
items.description: CoChat is fundamentally an AI collaboration platform designed for teamwork and to bring visibility and governance into enterprise AI shadows. The post CoChat Launches AI Collaboration Platform to Combat Shadow AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability
items.description: AI's danger isn't that it's creating new bugs, it's that it's amplifying old ones.

items.title: Ukraine confirms suspected APT28 campaign targeting prosecutors, anti-corruption agencies
items.description: The intrusions exploited vulnerabilities in the open-source Roundcube webmail platform that allow attackers to execute malicious code when a victim simply opens an email in their inbox.

items.title: Inside an Underground Guide: How Threat Actors Vet Stolen Credit Card Shops
items.description: In cybercrime markets, trust isn't assumed, it's verified. Flare reveals how underground guides teach actors to evaluate carding shops based on data quality, reputation, and survivability. [...]

items.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
items.description: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun, and UnDefend, all of which were released as zero-days by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse (

items.title: In defeat for Trump, House extends electronic spying program for just 10 days
items.description: The House passed stopgap legislation to extend a warrantless government surveillance power for 10 days, following a failed lobbying campaign by the Trump administration.

items.title: Coast Guard's New Cybersecurity Rules Offers Lessons for CISOs
items.description: The Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) requires plans to protect OT systems, audits by independent third parties, and a hybrid OT-security role.

items.title: Webinar: From phishing to fallout — Why MSPs must rethink both security and recovery
items.description: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...]

items.title: In Other News: Satellite Cybersecurity Act, $90K Chrome Flaw, Teen Hacker Arrested
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ShinyHunters targets Rockstar Games, ShowDoc vulnerability exploited in the wild, and EPA to boost cybersecurity budget to $19 million. The post In Other News: Satellite Cybersecurity Act, $90K Chrome Flaw, Teen Hacker Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
items.description: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and suspended 24.9 million accounts in 2025. The new policy updates relate to contact and location permissions in Android, allowing third-party apps to access the contact lists and a user's location in

items.title: Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison
items.description: Kamerin Stokes sold stolen credentials through an online marketplace even after pleading guilty to his role in the DraftKings attack. The post Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Followed
items.description: Thursday’s discussion comes as leaders on Capitol Hill grapple with the dizzying pace of global developments in which technology plays a central role. The post Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Followed appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: The remote code execution vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-34197 came to light in early April. The post Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
items.description: CISA warned that attackers are now exploiting a high-severity Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability, which was patched earlier this month after going undetected for 13 years. [...]

items.title: Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US
items.description: Kejia Wang and Zhenxing Wang compromised the identities of dozens of US persons to help land jobs at over 100 companies. The post Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities
items.description: The malware is configured to operate on systems associated with Israeli water treatment and desalination plants. The post ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft: Some Windows servers enter reboot loops after April patches
items.description: Microsoft warns that some Windows domain controllers are entering restart loops after installing the April 2026 security updates. [...]

items.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices
items.description: An indirect prompt injection could be chained with a sandbox bypass and Cursor’s remote tunnel feature for shell access to machines. The post Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it will only enrich those that fulfil certain conditions owing to an explosion in CVE submissions. "CVEs that do not meet those criteria will still be listed in the NVD but will not

items.title: Man gets 30 months for selling thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts
items.description: 23-year-old Kamerin Stokes of Memphis, Tennessee, was sentenced to 30 months in prison for selling access to tens of thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts. [...]

items.title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
items.description: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than 75,000 cybercriminals. The ongoing effort, dubbed Operation PowerOFF, disrupted access to the DDoS-for-hire services, took down the technical infrastructure supporting them, and obtained access to

items.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
items.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To that end, the agency has added the vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34197 (CVSS score: 8.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian

items.description: Introduction

items.title: NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.

items.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
items.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

items.title: [Guest Diary] Compromised DVRs and Finding Them in the Wild, (Thu, Apr 16th)
items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Alec Jaffe, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].

items.title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
items.description: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.

items.title: Newly Discovered PowMix Botnet Hits Czech Workers Using Randomized C2 Traffic
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2025. "PowMix employs randomized command-and-control (C2) beaconing intervals, rather than persistent connection to the C2 server, to evade the network signature detections," Cisco Talos

items.title: New Jersey men given lengthy sentences for running North Korean laptop farms
items.description: The DOJ said Kejia Wang, 42, was sentenced to nine years in prison and Zhenxing Wang, 39, was given a nearly eight-year sentence for an operation that generated more than $5 million for the government of North Korea.

items.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
items.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world.

items.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
items.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
items.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for. Not

items.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
items.description: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

items.title: Scanning for AI Models, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: Starting March 10, 2026, my DShield sensor started getting probe for various AI models such as claude, openclaw, huggingface, etc. Reviewing the data already reported by other DShield sensors to ISC, the DShield database shows reporting of these probes started that day and has been active ever since.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday April 2026., (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: This month&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft Patch Tuesday looks like a record one, but let&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s look at it a bit closer to understand what is happening

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, April 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9890, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Scans for EncystPHP Webshell, (Mon, Apr 13th)
items.description: Last week, I wrote about attackers scanning for various webshells, hoping to find some that do not require authentication or others that use well-known credentials. But some attackers are paying attention and are deploying webshells with more difficult-to-guess credentials. Today, I noticed some scans for what appears to be the "EncystPHP" web shell. Fortinet wrote about this webshell back in January. It appears to be a favorite among attackers compromising vulnerable FreePBX systems.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, April 13th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9888, (Mon, Apr 13th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
items.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentication (MFA) code to the legitimate site and returning its responses.

items.title: CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
CISA, UK NCSC, FBI Unveil Principles to Combat Cyber Risks in OT
CISA Retires Ten Emergency Directives, Marking an Era in Federal Cybersecurity
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
